name: Simons Summer Research Program

cost.notes: Completely FREE — all expenses covered including stipend

essays.prompt: Describe your scientific research interests and a specific question you might want to explore.
tips: Be specific about a question, not just a field
Show you understand what real research looks like

outcomes.college_impact: One of the strongest research program signals for MIT, Caltech, Stanford, and top research universities. RSI alumni frequently attend MIT.
outcomes.typical_results: Most alumni pursue STEM majors at top-20 research universities; many publish papers from their summer work.

what_they_look_for: Ability to work in a lab/research environment

blurbs.short: A free 6-week fully-funded research apprenticeship at Stony Brook University for rising juniors and seniors interested in science and math.
blurbs.long: STEMCO (Simons Summer Research Program) is one of the most prestigious and selective high school research programs in the country — and it's completely free. Students are paired with a faculty mentor at Stony Brook University and conduct real research over 6 weeks. Unlike some programs that offer pre-designed experiments, STEMCO students work on genuine research questions. The program covers physics, biology, chemistry, computer science, and applied mathematics. STEMCO alumni frequently cite the program as transformative, and the free nature of the program (plus a stipend) makes it accessible to students regardless of financial background. The application is extremely competitive — Stony Brook-affiliated schools tend to have higher admission rates, but the program actively recruits nationally.

curriculum: Independent laboratory research under faculty mentorship, weekly seminars, research methodology training, end-of-program poster session.
